sourceMovie.title: Thor: Love and Thunder
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Thor: Love and Thunder works as a fantasy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The fantasy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shazam! Fury of the Gods
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shazam! Fury of the Gods (2023) is the kind of film Thor: Love and Thunder fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The fantasy overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Doctor Strange
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Doctor Strange shares Thor: Love and Thunder's gift for fantasy — the 2016 film delivers action without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Thor
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Thor: Love and Thunder compelling — the fantasy, the action — shows up in Thor (2011) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Thor: Ragnarok
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action energy of Thor: Love and Thunder is alive in Thor: Ragnarok (2017), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Thor: The Dark World
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Thor: The Dark World (2013) operates in the same fantasy as Thor: Love and Thunder, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings (2021) belongs to the same conversation as Thor: Love and Thunder — both anchor their fantasy in action emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Thor: Love and Thunder's fantasy stayed with you, Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness (2022) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The action through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Avengers: Infinity War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Thor: Love and Thunder excels in action, Avengers: Infinity War (2018) doubles down on adventure to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014) is the natural next step after Thor: Love and Thunder: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and science-fiction elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Captain America: Civil War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Thor: Love and Thunder who respond to its action will find Captain America: Civil War (2016) immediately familiar. Both films share a adventure sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

faq.question: What should I watch after Thor: Love and Thunder?
faq.answer: Try Shazam! Fury of the Gods, Doctor Strange, Thor—each shares DNA with Thor: Love and Thunder while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Thor: Love and Thunder?
faq.answer: Thor: Love and Thunder (2022) is best described as Fantasy, Action, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Thor: Love and Thunder part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Thor Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
